mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce discount rates for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer demand, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand recovery, reinforcing corporate revenue growth expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDP and retail sales fails to predict positive 60-day returns (out-of-sample accuracy < 55%).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production index values indicate strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which translates to higher short-term corporate earnings momentum and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Industrial production increases consistently coincide with negative 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month CPI increase exceeding 0.6 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above the threshold consistently precede positive 20-day returns without subsequent rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent job gains and falling unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening, boosting consumer spending confidence and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising payrolls and falling unemployment fails to predict positive returns (Rank IC < 0.01) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts reflect strong household balance sheets and construction sector activity, which propagates through supply chains and supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts trends show zero predictive power for 60-day equity returns (t-statistic < 1.5) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling industrial production and rising consumer prices signals stagflationary supply shocks, eroding profit margins and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Stagflationary divergence patterns consistently precede positive 5-day returns without margin compression.
